message.content: Adversaries may exploit resource-intensive features of applications to cause an Endpoint Denial of Service (DoS) through Application Exhaustion Flood. This technique involves repeatedly requesting specific, resource-heavy features of web applications, leading to the exhaustion of system resources and ultimately denying access to the application or server. The impact of such an attack can be significant, disrupting business operations and causing potential financial losses. Common exploitation methods include automated scripts or botnets to generate a high volume of requests, overwhelming the target system. Mitigation strategies include implementing rate limiting, deploying web application firewalls (WAFs), optimizing application performance, and monitoring for unusual traffic patterns to detect and respond to potential DoS attacks promptly.
